name: Fresh Salsa (Chile)
recipeIngredient: juice from one lime or lemon
recipeInstructions: If you have access to a molcajete, use it for a more authentic texture. Otherwise, use a blender to achieve a waterier consistency. Toast the serrano peppers in a hot skillet, turning them occasionally until they blister evenly and develop charred patches on all sides. Remove the papery outer skin of the tomatillos and simmer them gently until they change from green to yellowish olive green (approximately 5 minutes). Mince or mash the garlic cloves. Combine the toasted peppers, tomatillos, garlic, tomatoes, cilantro, salt, and lime or lemon juice in a bowl, adjusting seasoning as desired. Variations: Add half an onion for depth of flavor. Use a mix of hot peppers such as jalapenos and Thai chilies. Removing seeds after toasting will reduce heat intensity. Generally, smaller chiles contribute more heat than larger ones.
